description: GKG indexer dashboard. Top-of-page rows tell the story (health, volume, throughput, latency, reliability, freshness). Bottom rows are the per-domain catalog reference and are collapsed by default.

panels.description: Share of repository indexing runs that ended in outcome=indexed over the last hour. Renders 0 when no indexing runs happened in the window.
panels.title: Code: success rate (1h)

panels.description: SDLC pipeline errors divided by rows processed, last hour. Lower is better.
panels.title: SDLC: error rate (1h)

panels.description: Highest current watermark-to-now lag across all SDLC entities. Rising means SDLC is falling behind.
panels.title: SDLC: max watermark lag

panels.description: p95 of end-to-end NATS message dispatch duration over the last 5 minutes.
panels.title: Indexer: message p95 (5m)

panels.description: Successful repository indexing runs in the dashboard window. Click the arrow to open the per-outcome rate in Explore; hover the strip below for the rate at a point in time.
panels.title: Projects indexed

panels.description: Push events processed by the code indexing handler in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Push events

panels.description: Source files seen by the code-graph indexer in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Files parsed

panels.description: Graph nodes and edges indexed by the code handler in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Nodes and edges

panels.description: Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rows extracted and written by SDLC pipelines in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Rows ingested

panels.description: Bytes returned by ClickHouse datalake extraction queries in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Bytes from datalake

panels.description: Total SDLC pipeline runs across all entities in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Pipeline runs

panels.description: Total SDLC pipeline failures in the dashboard window.
panels.title: Pipeline errors

panels.description: Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
Rate of change over time. Hover for exact values; click the link in the header for the full Explore view.
SDLC pipeline rows processed per Grafana auto-window, drawn as a smoothed trend line per entity.
panels.title: SDLC: rows ingested over time

panels.description: Repository indexing runs, stacked by outcome.
panels.title: Code: projects indexed over time

panels.description: Code-graph parse and analysis duration percentiles. Watch p95 climbing without p50 moving for a long-tail bottleneck.
panels.title: Code: time to index a project (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: SDLC pipeline duration percentiles, broken down by entity. The histogram does not carry a GitLab-namespace label today, so entity is the closest available dimension. Use the legend filter to isolate one entity if the panel gets busy.
panels.title: SDLC: pipeline duration by entity (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Time downloading a repository archive from Gitaly. A slow tail here often explains slow code indexing.
panels.title: Code: Gitaly fetch duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Per-entity p50, p95, and p99 over the dashboard time range. Sorted by p95 descending.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (entity, le) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[$__range]))))
topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (entity, le) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[$__range]))))
topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (entity, le) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[$__range]))))

panels.title: SDLC: top 10 slowest entities by p95

panels.description: Task-level code indexing failures in rolling 1h windows, stacked by stage. Increments only when a code indexing task ends with a fatal pipeline error (sink write, thread pool, sentinel, internal panic). Per-file failures are charted in `Code: per-file faults by kind` below.
panels.title: Code: task-level errors by pipeline stage (1h windows)

panels.description: Per-file failures during code indexing, stacked by kind. The task itself completes; individual files were excluded from the graph. Compare against the task-level errors panel above.
panels.title: Code: per-file faults by kind (1h windows)

panels.description: SDLC pipeline errors over rows processed, per entity. The 1h rate window is wide enough that sporadic errors still register; a tighter window goes to "No data" between bursts.

targets.expr: (((sum by (entity) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_errors_total{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[1h]))) or (sum by (entity) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_rows_processed_total{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[1h]))) * 0)) / (sum by (entity) (rate(gkg_indexer_sdlc_pipeline_rows_processed_total{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[1h])))

panels.title: SDLC: error rate by entity (1h window)

panels.description: Projects skipped at fetch time because the repository was terminal-empty. Often the explanation for "missing" projects.
panels.title: Code: empty-repo short-circuits

panels.description: SDLC pipeline errors counted in rolling 1h windows, stacked by error_kind. Falls back to a flat zero line during error-free windows so the panel never goes to "No data".
panels.title: SDLC: errors by kind (1h windows)

panels.description: Seconds between the per-entity SDLC watermark and now. Rising lag means SDLC is falling behind on that entity.
panels.title: SDLC: watermark lag per entity

panels.description: Active worker permits by kind. Watch the global pool flatlining at the configured ceiling, that is the bottleneck signal.
panels.title: ETL: worker permits in flight

panels.description: Aggregate CPU seconds used per second divided by configured CPU limit, across all indexer replicas. 1.0 means the pool is fully consuming its quota.

targets.expr: ((sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster", cpu="total"}[5m]))) / (sum(kube_pod_container_resource_limits{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster", namespace="gkg", resource="cpu"}) > 0)) or vector(0)

panels.title: CPU: usage / limit (5m)

panels.description: Working-set memory across all replicas as a fraction of the aggregate memory limit. Watch for sustained values above ~0.85 — that is OOM-kill territory.

targets.expr: ((sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"})) / (sum(kube_pod_container_resource_limits{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster", namespace="gkg", resource="memory"}) > 0)) or vector(0)

panels.title: Memory: working set / limit

panels.description: Total OOM-killer events across indexer replicas in the last hour. Any non-zero value warrants a look at the memory panel below.
panels.title: OOM events (1h)

panels.description: Container restarts across indexer replicas in the last hour. Crash-loops show up here before they show up in the app metrics.
panels.title: Restarts (1h)

panels.description: Per-pod CPU seconds consumed per second. The dashed reference line is the per-pod CPU limit from kube-state-metrics; pods bumping against it should be cross-checked with the throttling panel.
panels.title: CPU: cores used per pod

panels.description: Share of CFS periods where the cgroup was throttled. Anything sustained above ~5% means the pod is hitting its CPU quota and pipelines will queue.
panels.title: CPU: throttled time fraction per pod

panels.description: container_memory_working_set_bytes per replica. The dashed reference line is the per-pod memory limit. Working set is what the OOM killer reads, not RSS.
panels.title: Memory: working set per pod

panels.description: Resident set and page cache, per pod. RSS dominating with a tiny cache often means heap growth; cache dominating typically means Gitaly archive I/O.
panels.title: Memory: RSS and cache per pod

panels.description: Read and write throughput from cAdvisor. A sustained climb on writes during code indexing usually points at archive extraction; reads spike during ClickHouse query bursts.
panels.title: Filesystem I/O bytes per pod

panels.description: PSI seconds per second. IO pressure rising means processes are waiting on disk; memory pressure rising means the kernel is reclaiming pages, often the leading edge of an OOM.
panels.title: Pressure stall: IO and memory

panels.description: OS-level concurrency counters. Threads climbing without a corresponding workload increase usually points at a tokio blocking-pool growth event; sockets climbing flags a NATS or ClickHouse connection leak.
panels.title: Threads and sockets per pod

panels.description: Per-scope coverage of the migrating schema version. SDLC reaching 100% triggers promotion. Code coverage is informational. Currently dark in prod, see #524.
panels.title: Migration: indexed / eligible coverage

panels.description: Wall-clock seconds since the current migrating version was marked. Flat zero when no migration is active. Currently dark in prod, see #524.
panels.title: Migration: migrating-version age

panels.description: Total schema migration phase executions, labelled by phase and result.
panels.title: gkg_schema_migration_phase_total — rate/s

panels.description: Total rows written to ClickHouse per table.
panels.title: gkg_etl_destination_rows_written_total — rate/s

panels.description: Time to write a batch to ClickHouse.
panels.title: gkg_etl_destination_write_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: Time to write a batch to ClickHouse.
panels.title: gkg_etl_destination_write_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: Total failed writes to ClickHouse per table.
panels.title: gkg_etl_destination_write_errors_total — rate/s

panels.description: Total bytes written to ClickHouse per table.
panels.title: gkg_etl_destination_written_bytes_total — rate/s

panels.description: Time inside each handler's handle() call.
panels.title: gkg_etl_handler_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: Time inside each handler's handle() call.
panels.title: gkg_etl_handler_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: Total handler errors at the engine dispatch level.
panels.title: gkg_etl_handler_errors_total — rate/s

panels.description: End-to-end time per message through dispatch.
panels.title: gkg_etl_message_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: End-to-end time per message through dispatch.
panels.title: gkg_etl_message_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: ETL messages processed by the indexer, labelled by topic and outcome.
panels.title: gkg_etl_messages_processed_total — rate/s

panels.description: Time to fetch a batch of messages from NATS.
panels.title: gkg_etl_nats_fetch_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: Time to fetch a batch of messages from NATS.
panels.title: gkg_etl_nats_fetch_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: Time waiting for a worker pool permit, labelled by permit kind (global|group) and group.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le, permit_kind, group) (rate(gkg_etl_permit_wait_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[5m])))
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, permit_kind, group) (rate(gkg_etl_permit_wait_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[5m])))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, permit_kind, group) (rate(gkg_etl_permit_wait_duration_seconds_bucket{container="gkg-indexer", cluster=~"$cluster"}[5m])))

panels.title: gkg_etl_permit_wait_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: Time waiting for a worker pool permit, labelled by permit kind (global|group) and group.
panels.title: gkg_etl_permit_wait_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: Number of worker permits currently held, labelled by permit kind (global or concurrency-group name).
panels.title: gkg_etl_permits_active

panels.description: Total bytes that the archive extractor refused to write to disk, summed from the tar header sizes of skipped entries. Same `reason` labels as `gkg.indexer.code.archive.entries.skipped`.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_archive_bytes_skipped_bytes_total — rate/s

panels.description: Tar archive entries dropped before extraction so they never touch disk. Reasons: `excluded_extension` (basename matches the binary-asset / media / archive denylist in `code-graph::v2::config::filter::EXCLUDED_INDEXING_GLOBS`), `oversize` (declared size exceeds the configured per-file ceiling).
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_archive_entries_skipped_total — rate/s

panels.description: Task-level code indexing failures by pipeline stage. Increments only when a code indexing task ends with a fatal pipeline error (sink write, thread pool, sentinel spawn, internal panic). Per-file failures land in `gkg.indexer.code.file_faults` instead, so this rate is suitable for alerting.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_errors_total — rate/s

panels.description: Total push events processed by the code indexing handler.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_events_processed_total — rate/s

panels.description: Per-file failures during code indexing, by kind. The task itself completes successfully; individual files were excluded from the graph. Kinds: `file_read`, `invalid_utf8`, `syntax_error`, `oxc_panic`, `oxc_semantic`, `analyzer_panic`, `unknown_source_type`, `embedded_script_parse`, `rust_workspace_missing`.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_file_faults_total — rate/s

panels.description: Total files seen by the code-graph indexer.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_files_processed_total — rate/s

panels.description: Source files skipped by the code-graph indexer for policy or watchdog reasons. Not an error. Reasons: `oversize`, `oversize_combined`, `line_too_long`, `minified`, `not_utf8`, `non_regular_file`, `unsafe_path`, `timeout_sentinel`.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_files_skipped_total — rate/s

panels.description: End-to-end duration of processing a single push event.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_handler_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: End-to-end duration of processing a single push event.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_handler_duration_seconds — observation rate

panels.description: Duration of code-graph parsing and analysis.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_indexing_duration_seconds — p50/p95/p99

panels.description: Duration of code-graph parsing and analysis.
panels.title: gkg_indexer_code_indexing_duration_seconds — observation rate

title: Orbit — GKG indexer
